documents.title: Code of Conduct
documents.summary: CosmicSec exists to advance **ethical cybersecurity** — protecting systems, educating defenders, and empowering authorized security professionals. As contributors and maintainers, we pledge to make participation in this project a respectful, harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of background, experience level, or identity.

documents.title: Contributing to CosmicSec
documents.summary: Thank you for your interest in contributing to CosmicSec! We welcome contributions that advance **ethical cybersecurity research, education, and authorized security tooling**. By contributing, you agree to uphold the ethical standards described in our [LICENSE](LICENSE) and [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md).

documents.title: Security Policy
documents.summary: CosmicSec is a cybersecurity platform. We take security seriously and are committed to responsible disclosure. If you discover a vulnerability in CosmicSec itself, please follow the process below.

documents.title: CosmicSec CLI Agent
documents.summary: The CosmicSec local agent discovers and runs security tools on your machine, streams findings to the CosmicSec cloud platform, and supports full offline operation with automatic sync on reconnect.

documents.title: CosmicSec Agent CLI
documents.summary: CosmicSec Agent is a local-first security automation CLI that discovers tools on your machine, executes scans, and syncs results to CosmicSec cloud when available.

documents.title: Deploy CosmicSec to Production
documents.summary: - Docker 24+ and Docker Compose 2.20+ - PostgreSQL 16 instance (managed or self-hosted) - Redis 7+ instance - Domain name with DNS control - TLS certificate (Let's Encrypt recommended)

documents.title: CosmicSec Incident Response Runbook
documents.summary: | Level | Response Time | Examples | |-------|--------------|---------| | **P1 — Critical** | 15 min | Service completely down, data breach, auth bypass | | **P2 — High** | 1 hour | Performance degradation >50%, partial outage | | **P3 — Medium** | 4 hours | Single feature broken, minor data inconsistency | | **P4 — Low** | Next business day | UI glitch, non-critical feature degraded |
